verdict: 2026-06-26 11:50:12 PDT — The implementation shape is otherwise sound: it uses Railway's existing declarative `startCommand` to make node PID 1, keeps the already-correct shutdown handler, reuses the existing config-contract test with exact equality, and avoids adding an init/process-manager dependency or Docker gate. I attempted `npm test`, but the read-only sandbox blocked Vitest's temp config write; that is not evidence against the branch.

findings.title: Committed review artifact violates the scoped file set
findings.claim: The branch adds `reviews/railway-pid1-shutdown.design.json`, a raw Codex design-review output, even though AC6 says `git diff --name-only main...HEAD` should show no files beyond `railway.json`, `server/test/health.test.js`, `server/test/shutdown.test.js`, `reviews/graceful-shutdown.md`, and the story file. It also duplicates review/disposition state that already lives in `reviews/railway-pid1-shutdown.md`, creating a second nonstandard source of truth.
findings.alternative: Delete `reviews/railway-pid1-shutdown.design.json` and keep the design-review/disposition record only in `reviews/railway-pid1-shutdown.md`.
findings.win: Restores AC6 exactly, removes an unowned generated artifact from the branch, and leaves one canonical review record for future rounds.
